description_long: This is a military-style attack airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and twin turbofan engines mounted on pylons above the rear fuselage. The fuselage is robust and streamlined, with a forward-positioned, enclosed cockpit featuring a large canopy for enhanced visibility. The wings are equipped with multiple hardpoints for external stores, such as missiles or bombs, and display military insignia. The tail section features a distinctive twin vertical stabilizer connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. The overall surface is matte gray, typical of military aircraft, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted or not modeled.
description: A gray, military attack airplane with high-mounted straight wings, twin rear turbofan engines, and a forward enclosed cockpit. It features an H-tail and multiple hardpoints under the wings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the straight, high-mounted wings, forward cockpit, and twin engines above the fuselage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit placement, and the twin engine nacelles above the rear fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the rear-mounted engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the wing hardpoints and overall aircraft profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with multiple hardpoints and no visible winglets; slight anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turbofan engines mounted above the rear fuselage on pylons.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a large canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
